1. Executive Summary

419
Companies with CFO Data
$5.7M
Median CFO Total Comp
$7.2M
Mean CFO Total Comp
208
CFOs with Equity Holdings Data
Key findings:
  • The median S&P 500 CFO earns $5.7M in total reported compensation — roughly 40-50% of the median CEO comp for the same companies.
  • CFO total comp ranges from $358,625 to $139.5M, a 389x spread.
  • Stock awards are the largest component for most CFOs, comprising 40-60% of total comp at most companies.
  • CFO turnover is significant: 275 companies show CFO transitions in the 2022-2025 period.

2. Methodology

This report analyzes CFO compensation data extracted from 4077 SEC DEF 14A proxy statements filed by 526 S&P 500 companies between 2023 and 2026. CFOs are identified by title matching in the Summary Compensation Table (SCT), looking for "Chief Financial Officer", "CFO", and related titles.

Equity ownership calculations: Outstanding equity holdings (unvested RSUs, unearned PSUs, exercisable and unexercisable options) are extracted from the "Outstanding Equity Awards at Fiscal Year-End" table. Beneficial ownership data comes from the "Security Ownership" section. Equity values are calculated using current stock prices from yfinance.

Interactive features: Tables with ⇅ column headers are sortable — click any header to sort. Rows with ▶ arrows are expandable — click to see the full multi-year compensation breakdown with component mix visualizations.

Limitations: Not all companies include CFOs in their proxy tables with clearly identified titles. Some companies use combined titles (e.g., "President and CFO") or interim designations. Former CFOs who departed mid-year may have partial-year compensation that appears artificially low.

3. CFO Compensation Overview

All 419 companies with CFO data, sorted by total compensation (most recent filing). Click any row to expand the full multi-year compensation breakdown by component.

Click any row to see salary, bonus, stock, options, incentive pay, pension, and other compensation for up to 3 fiscal years, with visual breakdowns. Column headers are sortable.

9. CFO Mega-Equity Awards (>$10M)

Single-year CFO equity awards exceeding $10 million. These often coincide with promotions, retention packages, or company transformations.

180 CFO equity awards exceeding $10M were identified. The largest is Vaibhav Taneja at TSLA ($139.2M in 2024).

10. Key Patterns & Conclusions

Summary of CFO Compensation Patterns

PatternDescriptionData Point
Equity Dominance Most CFOs receive >50% of comp in equity 311 of 408 CFOs (76%)
CEO/CFO Gap Median CEO earns ~2.8x the CFO Range: 0.0x to 44.3x
CFO Turnover Significant turnover in reporting period 275 companies had CFO changes
Mega-Grants Rare CFO equity awards >$10M are uncommon 180 instances identified
Salary Floor CFO base salary is more meaningful vs. CEO 9 CFOs (2%) have salary >30% of total

Key Observations

  1. CFO pay is more standardized than CEO pay: The spread between highest and lowest-paid CFOs ($139.5M/$358,625 = 389x) is much narrower than the CEO spread, reflecting the more uniform nature of the CFO role across industries.
  2. Stock awards dominate CFO comp: Averaging 58% of total, stock awards (RSUs + PSUs) are the single largest component for most CFOs.
  3. CEO/CFO ratio reflects governance philosophy: Companies with near-parity ratios (1.0-1.5x) tend to have co-equal C-suite structures, while high ratios (>5x) often indicate founder-CEO premium or mega-grant distortions.
  4. Equity ownership varies enormously: Some CFOs hold equity worth tens of millions from accumulated grants and vesting, while others at similarly-sized companies have minimal holdings, reflecting differences in tenure, grant practices, and personal selling decisions.
Note on data limitations: CFO identification relies on title matching in proxy filings. Companies with combined titles (e.g., "President and CFO") or interim CFOs may produce inconsistent matches. Equity values use current market prices and are point-in-time estimates. Performance share values assume target payout (1x). Option values only include in-the-money intrinsic value and do not account for time value.
CFO Compensation Report — Generated February 28, 2026 at 10:33 PM
Data source: SEC EDGAR DEF 14A proxy statements (526 companies, 4077 filings, 2018-2026)
Stock prices: yfinance, February 28, 2026
